mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust supply-side growth, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises by >1% MoM but 20-day SPX returns are negative in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% indicates tight labor markets, which boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE < 5% but 5-day SPX returns are negative in >40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong aggregate demand, which drives equity valuations higher over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 and INDPRO both rise but 60-day SPX returns are negative in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2% YoY) forces central banks to tighten policy, creating headwinds for equity markets and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 2% but 20-day SPX returns are positive in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST rises by >2% MoM but 5-day SPX returns are negative in >40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A Fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4% signals monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS > 4% but 60-day SPX returns are positive in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a favorable environment for equity markets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS rises and FEDFUNDS falls but 20-day SPX returns are negative in >40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Surging retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS rises by >3% MoM but 20-day SPX returns are negative in >50% of cases.
